scenarios.name: Specific Performance of Sale Agreement

checks.detail: Specific Relief Act / specific performance cited
At least one statutory section cited
Prayer for execution of sale deed
No invented amounts (10L/50L never in facts)

scenarios.draft_text:
IN THE COURT OF THE CITY CIVIL COURT
AT BENGALURU

O.S. No. __________ / 20____

Anita Sharma,
Aged about 52 years,
Occupation: {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: Indiranagar, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... PLAINTIFF

                    VERSUS

Rajesh Reddy,
Aged about 60 years,
Occupation: {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: HSR Layout, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... DEFENDANT

SUIT FOR SPECIFIC PERFORMANCE OF CONTRACT RELATING TO IMMOVABLE PROPERTY WITH INTEREST AND COSTS

MOST RESPECTFULLY SHOWETH:

JURISDICTION

1. The Plaintiff is a person sui juris and competent to institute the present suit. The Defendant is also a person sui juris and can be sued.

2. This Hon'ble Court has territorial jurisdiction to try and entertain the present suit inasmuch as the cause of action arose within the local limits of the jurisdiction of this Hon'ble Court at Bengaluru, and the Defendant carries on business / resides within such territorial limits.

3. This Hon'ble Court has pecuniary jurisdiction to try the present suit, the suit being valued at Rs. {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}}/- (Rupees {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE_WORDS}} Only), which is within the pecuniary limits prescribed for this Hon'ble Court.

LIMITATION

4. The present suit is within the period of limitation prescribed under Article 54 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963, which provides a period of three years from date fixed for performance, or if no date fixed, when plaintiff has notice that performance is refused. The suit is filed well within the prescribed limitation period.

FACTS OF THE CASE

5. On 10.01.2024, the Plaintiff and the Defendant entered into an Agreement to Sell in respect of the residential property bearing Flat No. 302, 3rd Floor, Sunshine Apartments, Koramangala, Bengaluru, measuring 1200 sq ft. (Annexure A).

6. The total consideration agreed upon for the said property was Rs. 85,00,000/-. On the date of the agreement, the Plaintiff paid an advance amount of Rs. 25,00,000/- to the Defendant via RTGS bearing UTR No. HDFC20240110987654. (Annexure B).

7. As per the terms of the Agreement, the balance consideration of Rs. 60,00,000/- was to be paid by the Plaintiff on or before 10.07.2024 against the execution and registration of the Sale Deed.

8. The Plaintiff has always been ready and willing to perform her part of the contract and remains so to date.

BREACH OF CONTRACT

9. On 05.06.2024, the Defendant sent a letter to the Plaintiff expressly refusing to execute the Sale Deed, stating that he had received a higher offer for the said property. (Annexure C).

10. Consequently, the Plaintiff issued a legal notice dated 20.06.2024 calling upon the Defendant to execute the Sale Deed in terms of the Agreement. (Annexure D).

11. The Defendant has failed and refused to comply with the said notice and has not executed the Sale Deed despite the Plaintiff's tender of readiness and willingness.

DAMAGES

12. Due to the Defendant's wrongful refusal and breach of contract, the Plaintiff has suffered loss and inconvenience, including costs incurred towards legal proceedings and mental agony.

13. The Plaintiff claims compensation for the breach of contract, the exact quantification of which is reserved to be determined by this Hon'ble Court at the time of final hearing.

LEGAL BASIS

14. The present cause is governed by Specific Relief Act, 1963, including Section 10 (mandatory after 2018 Amendment), Section 16, and Section 22.

15. The present cause is governed by Indian Contract Act, 1872, including Section 10.

16. The Plaintiff also relies on Transfer of Property Act, 1882, including Section 53A, where the pleaded facts attract those provisions.

CAUSE OF ACTION

17. The cause of action for the present suit first arose on 05.06.2024 when the Defendant committed the breach / wrongful act giving rise to the Plaintiff's right to institute the present suit.

18. The cause of action further arose when the Defendant, despite receipt of the legal notice dated 20.06.2024, failed and refused to comply with the Plaintiff's demand.

19. The cause of action is not a continuing cause of action. The breach consisted of a single event. However, the Plaintiff's right to damages subsists.

VALUATION AND COURT FEE

20. The suit is valued at Rs. {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}}/- (Rupees {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE_WORDS}} Only) for the purposes of jurisdiction and court fee. Appropriate court fee has been paid on this plaint under the provisions of the Court Fees Act, 1870 as applicable in the State of Karnataka. The plaint is properly stamped.

INTEREST

21. The Plaintiff claims interest on the suit amount as follows:

    (a) Pendente lite interest at such rate as this Hon'ble Court deems fit and just from the date of filing of this suit till the date of judgment / decree, under Section 34 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908;
    (b) Future interest at such rate as this Hon'ble Court deems fit and just from the date of judgment / decree till the date of realisation of the entire decretal amount.

PRAYER

In the premises aforesaid, the Plaintiff most respectfully prays that this Hon'ble Court be pleased to:

    (a) Pass a decree for specific performance of contract directing the Defendant to execute and register the Sale Deed in favour of the Plaintiff in respect of the suit property upon payment of the balance consideration of Rs. 60,00,000/-;
    (b) In the alternative, award compensation for breach of contract as quantified by this Hon'ble Court;
    (c) Award possession of the suit property upon execution of the Sale Deed;
    (d) Award pendente lite and future interest at such rate as this Hon'ble Court deems just under Section 34 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908;
    (e) Award costs of the suit including costs of the legal notice;
    (f) Grant such other and further relief(s) as this Hon'ble Court may deem fit and proper in the facts and circumstances of the case.

LIST OF DOCUMENTS

Annexure A — Original agreement dated 10.01.2024

Annexure B — RTGS receipt (UTR: HDFC20240110987654)

Annexure C — Defendant's refusal letter dated 05.06.2024

Annexure D — Legal notice dated 20.06.2024 with postal receipt

VERIFICATION

Verified at {{CITY}} on this the ____ day of __________ 20____.

I, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, the Plaintiff above-named, do hereby verify that the contents of paragraphs 1 to 21 of this plaint are true and correct to my knowledge, and no material facts have been concealed therefrom, and that I have not filed any other suit, petition, or proceeding in any court or before any authority in respect of the same subject matter and cause of action.

DEPONENT

{{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}

PLAINTIFF

                                    THROUGH:
                                    {{ADVOCATE_NAME}}
                                    Advocate for the Plaintiff
                                    Enrollment No. {{ENROLLMENT_NO}}
                                    {{ADVOCATE_ADDRESS}}
                                    Mobile: {{ADVOCATE_PHONE}}
                                    Email: {{ADVOCATE_EMAIL}}

scenarios.name: Breach of Contract — Damages (Business)

checks.detail: Total claim Rs.32L or breakdown stated

scenarios.draft_text:
IN THE COURT OF THE COMMERCIAL COURT (COMMERCIAL DIVISION)
AT BENGALURU

Commercial Suit No. __________ / 20____

TechSoft Solutions Pvt Ltd,
A company registered under the Companies Act,
Having its registered office at MG Road, Bengaluru,
Represented by its Director Priya Menon, aged 40 years.

                                                        ... PLAINTIFF

                    VERSUS

CloudBridge Systems LLP,
A limited liability partnership having its office at Whitefield, Bengaluru,
Represented by its Designated Partner Vikram Singh, aged 48 years.

                                                        ... DEFENDANT

SUIT FOR DAMAGES FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT WITH INTEREST AND COSTS

MOST RESPECTFULLY SHOWETH:

COMMERCIAL COURT MAINTAINABILITY

1. The subject matter of the present suit arises out of a commercial dispute within the meaning of Section 2(1)(c) of the Commercial Courts Act, 2015. The suit is valued above the specified value as prescribed under Section 12 of the Commercial Courts Act, 2015, and is therefore maintainable before this Hon'ble Commercial Court.

JURISDICTION

2. The Plaintiff is a person sui juris and competent to institute the present suit. The Defendant is also a person sui juris and can be sued.

3. This Hon'ble Court has territorial jurisdiction to try and entertain the present suit inasmuch as the cause of action arose within the local limits of the jurisdiction of this Hon'ble Court at Bengaluru, and the Defendant carries on business within such territorial limits.

4. This Hon'ble Court has pecuniary jurisdiction to try the present suit, the suit being valued at Rs. 32,00,000/- (Rupees Thirty-Two Lakhs Only), which is within the pecuniary limits prescribed for this Hon'ble Court. This Court has subject matter jurisdiction to entertain commercial disputes under the Commercial Courts Act, 2015.

LIMITATION

5. The present suit is within the period of limitation prescribed under Article 55 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963, which provides a period of three years from when the contract is broken. The suit is filed well within the prescribed limitation period.

SECTION 12A COMPLIANCE

6. The Plaintiff states that {{MEDIATION_COMPLIANCE_STATEMENT -- Insert: (a) Pre-institution mediation was conducted under Section 12A of Commercial Courts Act, certificate enclosed; OR (b) Urgent interim relief is sought, exempting mediation requirement}}.

FACTS OF THE CASE

7. The Plaintiff is TechSoft Solutions Pvt Ltd, a company registered under the Companies Act, having its registered office at MG Road, Bengaluru, represented by its Director Priya Menon. The Defendant is CloudBridge Systems LLP, a limited liability partnership having its office at Whitefield, Bengaluru, represented by its Designated Partner Vikram Singh.

8. On 01.02.2024, the Plaintiff and the Defendant entered into a Software Development Agreement for the development of an ERP system for a total contract value of Rs. 45,00,000/-. A copy of the said Agreement is marked as Annexure A.

9. Pursuant to the terms of the Agreement, the Plaintiff paid a sum of Rs. 20,00,000/- as the first milestone payment to the Defendant via NEFT on 01.02.2024, bearing UTR Number ICIC20240201456789. A copy of the NEFT receipt is marked as Annexure B.

10. As per the agreed timeline under the Agreement, the Defendant was contractually obligated to deliver the first module of the ERP system by 01.05.2024.

BREACH OF CONTRACT

11. The Defendant failed to deliver the first module or any part of the ERP system by the agreed deadline of 01.05.2024.

12. On 15.06.2024, the Defendant abandoned the project entirely without completing the agreed scope of work. Copies of the email correspondence evidencing the abandonment of the project are marked as Annexure C.

13. Despite the abandonment of the project and the failure to deliver the modules, the Defendant has failed and refused to refund the advance payment of Rs. 20,00,000/- paid by the Plaintiff.

14. The Plaintiff issued a legal notice to the Defendant dated 01.07.2024 calling upon the Defendant to refund the advance and compensate for the losses suffered. A copy of the legal notice is marked as Annexure E.

15. The Defendant failed to respond satisfactorily to the said legal notice dated 01.07.2024.

DAMAGES

16. The Plaintiff is entitled to the refund of the advance payment of Rs. 20,00,000/- paid to the Defendant, which constitutes the actual loss suffered due to the breach.

17. Due to the non-delivery of the ERP system and the subsequent delay in operations, the Plaintiff has suffered consequential losses quantified at Rs. 12,00,000/-. A copy of the loss assessment report prepared by the auditor is marked as Annexure D.

18. The Defendant is in breach of {{CLAUSE_NUMBERS}} of the Software Development Agreement dated 01.02.2024.

19. The consequential damages claimed are based on {{CALCULATION_METHOD}} as assessed by the Plaintiff's auditor.

20. The Plaintiff claims interest at the rate of 18% per annum on the total claimed amount of Rs. 32,00,000/- from the date of breach until realisation.

LEGAL BASIS

21. The Defendant has committed a repudiatory breach by refusing to perform its obligations, entitling the Plaintiff to treat the contract as at an end and seek appropriate relief.

22. The Plaintiff is entitled to compensation under Section 73 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872, which provides that when a contract has been broken, the party who suffers by such breach is entitled to receive compensation for any loss or damage caused thereby, which naturally arose in the usual course of things from such breach, or which the parties knew, when they made the contract, to be likely to result from the breach of it.

CAUSE OF ACTION

23. The cause of action for the present suit first arose on 15.06.2024 when the Defendant committed the breach giving rise to the Plaintiff's right to institute the present suit.

24. The cause of action further arose when the Defendant, despite receipt of the legal notice dated 01.07.2024, failed and refused to comply with the Plaintiff's demand.

25. The cause of action is not a continuing cause of action. The breach consisted of a single event. However, the Plaintiff's right to damages subsists.

VALUATION AND COURT FEE

26. The suit is valued at Rs. 32,00,000/- (Rupees Thirty-Two Lakhs Only) for the purposes of jurisdiction and court fee. Appropriate court fee has been paid on this plaint under the provisions of the Court Fees Act, 1870 as applicable in the State of Karnataka. The plaint is properly stamped.

INTEREST

27. The Plaintiff claims interest on the suit amount as follows:
    (a) Pendente lite interest at such rate as this Hon'ble Court deems fit and just from the date of filing of this suit till the date of judgment / decree, under Section 34 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908;
    (b) Future interest at such rate as this Hon'ble Court deems fit and just from the date of judgment / decree till the date of realisation of the entire decretal amount.

PRAYER

In the premises aforesaid, the Plaintiff most respectfully prays that this Hon'ble Court be pleased to:

    (a) Pass a decree in favour of the Plaintiff and against the Defendant for a sum of Rs. 32,00,000/- (Rupees Thirty-Two Lakhs Only) towards damages;
    (b) Award Rs. 20,00,000/- towards Actual Loss;
    (c) Award Rs. 12,00,000/- towards Consequential loss / damages;
    (d) Award pendente lite and future interest at such rate as this Hon'ble Court deems just under Section 34 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908;
    (e) Award costs of the suit including costs of the legal notice;
    (f) Grant such other and further relief(s) as this Hon'ble Court may deem fit and proper in the facts and circumstances of the case.

SCHEDULE OF DAMAGES / PARTICULARS OF DAMAGES

The Plaintiff claims the following heads of damages with computation methodology:

28. Actual Loss
   Amount claimed: Rs. 20,00,000/-
   Basis / methodology: Refund
